title: feat(workflows): allow scheduled workflows
body: Adds the ability to schedule workflows on a cron-like recurrence. New trigger type `schedule` joins `event` and `batch`. UI exposes a schedule picker; backend persists the recurrence on `HogFlow` and a worker fans the workflow out at each fire.
author: abheek

changedFiles.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,84 @@
+export function HogFlowSchedulePicker({ value, onChange }) {
+  return (
+    <LemonField name="schedule">
+      <CronInput value={value} onChange={onChange} />
+    </LemonField>
+  )
+}
+
+// fires hog_flow_created when the user persists the new flow
+// posthog.capture('hog_flow_created', { trigger_type: 'schedule' })

@@ -3,8 +3,14 @@
 const TRIGGER_TYPES = [
   { value: 'event', label: 'On event' },
   { value: 'batch', label: 'On batch' },
+  { value: 'schedule', label: 'On a schedule' },
 ]

@@ -45,3 +45,25 @@
 class HogFlow(models.Model):
     trigger_type = models.CharField(
         choices=[('event', 'Event'), ('batch', 'Batch'), ('schedule', 'Schedule')],
     )
+    schedule = models.JSONField(null=True, blank=True)
+
+    def is_scheduled(self) -> bool:
+        return self.trigger_type == 'schedule'

unifiedDiff: (see changedFiles[].patch — used directly by the fixture runner)
